name: IoT / edge device fleet
summary: A large population of resource-constrained field devices (sensors/actuators) reporting through edge gateways to a cloud backend, provisioned and managed remotely over intermittent networks. Distributed, long-lived, physically exposed hardware with over-the-air lifecycle management.

concerns: per-device unique identity and credentials (X.509 cert / hardware root of trust / secure element / TPM); no shared or default keys across the fleet
zero-touch / secure provisioning and onboarding; attestation before a device joins the fleet
secure boot, signed firmware, and rollback-protection so only authenticated images run
physical / tamper resistance: debug-port (JTAG/UART) lockdown, anti-rollback fuses, key extraction resistance
mutual TLS (or DTLS/PSK) for device-to-cloud transport; credential rotation and revocation of compromised devices
compromise containment: per-device keys so one breached device cannot impersonate or decrypt the fleet
questions: Is each device given a unique, hardware-anchored identity and credentials (no shared/default secrets)?
Are secure boot, firmware signing, and anti-rollback required, and is device attestation performed at onboarding?
Are credential rotation, revocation of compromised devices, and physical/tamper protections (debug lockdown) specified?

concerns: fleet-scale OTA firmware/config updates: staged/canary rollout, cohort targeting, bandwidth throttling, and automatic rollback on failed update
remote device management: reboot, factory reset, config push, quarantine, decommission at scale
device lifecycle: onboarding, active, suspended, RMA/return, end-of-life and key wipe
intermittent / offline connectivity as the normal case: store-and-forward, reconnection/backoff, cellular data-plan and roaming cost management
fleet observability: device health/heartbeat, connectivity state, firmware-version distribution, and alerting on abnormal cohorts
questions: Is fleet OTA specified with staged/canary rollout, cohort targeting, and automatic rollback on failure?
Is normal-case intermittent/offline connectivity handled via store-and-forward and reconnection strategy?
Are remote management, device lifecycle states, and end-of-life key-wipe/decommissioning defined?

concerns: a failed OTA must never brick a device: A/B (dual-bank) images, watchdogs, and recovery/failsafe partition
autonomous operation and graceful degradation during cloud/backend or network outage
unattended long-duration uptime with self-recovery (watchdog resets) without a truck-roll
data durability across power loss and disconnection so no telemetry is lost during outages
questions: Is bricking prevented via A/B images, watchdog, and a recoverable failsafe partition?
Is autonomous, degraded operation during backend/network outage specified?
Is telemetry durably buffered across power loss and reconnection so nothing is lost?

concerns: messaging/transport protocols and versions: MQTT (3.1.1/5.0), CoAP, HTTP, AMQP, LwM2M; QoS levels
radio/link technologies: Wi-Fi, BLE, cellular (LTE-M/NB-IoT), LoRaWAN, Zigbee/Thread and their gateways
heterogeneous device generations/hardware revisions coexisting under one backend (forward/backward compatibility of the message schema)
gateway ↔ cloud and device ↔ gateway interoperability; payload encoding (CBOR/Protobuf/JSON)
questions: Are the messaging protocols/versions and QoS levels (e.g. MQTT 5.0) specified?
Are the radio/link technologies and gateway topology defined?
Is coexistence of multiple hardware generations under one backend (schema back/forward compatibility) addressed?

concerns: edge compute limits: constrained CPU/RAM/flash, on-device inference/filtering budgets
telemetry ingestion at fleet scale: messages/sec, connection concurrency, and backend throughput/backpressure
energy/power budget: battery life targets, duty-cycling, sleep modes, and transmit-power vs longevity trade-offs
constrained-network efficiency: payload size, compression, and update-download size over metered links
questions: Are edge CPU/RAM/flash and on-device processing budgets specified?
Are fleet-scale ingestion throughput and concurrent-connection targets quantified?
Are power/battery-life and bandwidth/payload-efficiency budgets defined for constrained devices?

concerns: telemetry schema, units, timestamps, and time-series modelling; clock sync/skew across devices
sampling/aggregation strategy, edge pre-processing, and downsampling/retention tiers
buffering, deduplication, and out-of-order/late-arriving data reconciliation after reconnection
device shadow / digital twin state consistency between device and cloud
data provenance: which device/firmware/sensor produced each reading, and calibration metadata
questions: Is the telemetry schema (units, timestamps, clock-sync) defined for time-series data?
Are buffering, deduplication, and out-of-order/late-arrival handling specified after reconnection?
Is device-shadow/twin state consistency and per-reading provenance addressed?

concerns: remote diagnostics and log retrieval from field devices without physical access
firmware/config versioning, traceability, and reproducible builds per hardware revision
observability of the fleet: per-cohort health, error rates, and update-success telemetry
field-debuggability: safe remote log-level control and crash/coredump capture
questions: Can devices be diagnosed and logs retrieved remotely without a field visit?
Are firmware/config versioning and per-revision traceability specified?
Are fleet observability and update-success metrics defined?

concerns: multiple hardware platforms/SoCs and RTOS/embedded-Linux targets under one codebase
hardware-abstraction layer so the firmware ports across chip revisions and component substitutions (supply-chain part swaps)
packaging/flashing at manufacturing and re-flash in the field
questions: Are the target SoCs/RTOS/OS platforms and a hardware-abstraction boundary specified?
Is portability across chip revisions / component substitutions (supply-chain swaps) addressed?

concerns: actuating/physical devices must fail to a safe state on fault, power loss, or lost connectivity
hazardous mis-actuation prevention when commands arrive stale, duplicated, or out of order
environmental hazard tolerance (thermal, electrical) and safe operating limits
questions: For actuating devices, is a fail-safe state on fault, power loss, or lost connectivity defined?
Are stale/duplicated/out-of-order commands rejected to prevent hazardous actuation?

concerns: personal/behavioural data captured by field sensors (location, video, usage) and GDPR obligations
data residency for telemetry routed across regions/cloud regions
EU Cyber Resilience Act / consumer-IoT security-labelling and mandated support/patching lifetime obligations
questions: Is PII captured by sensors (location/video/usage) identified with consent and GDPR handling?
Are data-residency and regulatory security-labelling / mandated-patching-lifetime (e.g. CRA) obligations addressed?
